step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("role of Mcm2-Ctf4-Polα axis in histone transfer during DNA replication")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

do you agree or disagree with the prior conclusion?
(Write at most 2 sentences. Briefly state whether you agree (adding one new reason) or disagree (giving one counterpoint), based on evidence.)
State your final answer choice clearly using \boxed{{letter}} format.)
Based on the retrieved evidence, there is no mention of the Mcm2-Ctf4-Polα axis or its functional role in histone transfer during DNA replication. While histone synthesis and reassembly occur after DNA replication, the available sources indicate that DNA remains unassociated with histones during replication itself to facilitate enzyme activity, suggesting that histone loading happens independently of the core replisome components like Polα. Therefore, I agree with the prior conclusion that the Mcm2-Ctf4-Polα axis does not mediate histone transfer to the leading strand.
\boxed{\text{B}}
